summary:
Closed an upstream evidence-completeness gap in ai_router.verify_session (the 6-round-churn cause the SS1-SS3 integrity work did not cover). Fix A: generated-bundle excludes are now depth-agnostic (**/

glob pathspecs), so a NESTED bundle (tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/dist) is excluded like a top-level dist/ -- retiring the per-repo --exclude workaround -- with excluded TRACKED paths reported explicitly so nothing is silently dropped. Fix B: an oversized/truncated-INPUT guard raised from assemble_evidence itself (EvidenceTooLargeError, cap AI_ROUTER_VERIFY_MAX_EVIDENCE_CHARS default 600KiB) so every caller fails closed, mirroring SS3's output-truncation guard. Both git-pathspec behavior and the guard are proven by tests (56 in test_verify_session.py; full suite 2913 passed). Verification ran two rounds (gpt-5-4, cross-provider): round 1 found TWO real bugs (a src/dist completeness regression and Fix B not threaded through assemble_evidence), both fixed; round 2 CONFIRMS the fixes and raised only two verifier category errors (immutable-round-1-artifact + circular set-not-closed-at-verify-time). Per the bounded-round discipline the loop stopped at round 2; the operator adjudicated both residual findings as non-defects and authorized this operator-attested close. Verdict WAIVED (honest override, not VERIFIED).
